skill.name: geo-search-api
skill.description: Search for gene expression DataSets and Profiles in the NCBI GEO database. Use this skill when the user wants to find microarray, RNA-seq, or other genomic data by keywords, organism, author, or specific fields.
skill.summary: Search NCBI GEO for gene expression datasets and profiles by keyword, organism, author, and other biological filters.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 75 static analysis findings are false positives after evaluation. The skill makes legitimate HTTP requests to the NCBI E-utilities API (a public government bioinformatics service) and accepts user-provided email and API key credentials via CLI arguments rather than embedding secrets. Shell commands shown in SKILL.md are documentation code blocks in markdown, not executed code. No evidence of malicious intent, obfuscation, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was found. The skill is a standard NCBI GEO API client with well-documented behavior.

low_findings.title: User-provided API credentials accepted via CLI
low_findings.description: The script accepts --email and --api_key command-line arguments passed to NCBI E-utilities. These are user-provided credentials, not hardcoded secrets. NCBI recommends providing an email and API key for higher rate limits when accessing their public API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Accepting CLI-based credentials is standard for API client tools. No credentials are hardcoded or stored. The credentials are only sent to NCBI's official API endpoint.

low_findings.title: External HTTP requests to NCBI API
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTP requests to eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov using Python's built-in urllib. This is a legitimate, documented public API for bioinformatics data retrieval from the National Center for Biotechnology Information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API endpoint is the official NCBI E-utilities service, a government research resource. The BASE_URL is hardcoded to this single legitimate endpoint.

low_findings.title: Documentation shell commands in SKILL.md
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains backtick-delimited bash commands for setup and execution. These are static markdown code blocks documenting example usage, not executable code injected into runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All commands are inside markdown fenced code blocks showing example usage. SKILL.md is documentation only and is never executed as a script.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm pattern match
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged keywords related to cryptography (e.g., 'MIT' license, 'MD' file extensions, 'MESH' field alias). These are false positives from keyword matching on documentation text with no actual cryptographic usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic algorithms are implemented, imported, or invoked anywhere in this skill. The flagged text is license metadata ('MIT') and NCBI field name aliases ('MESH').

content.user_title: Search NCBI GEO for Gene Expression Datasets and Profiles
content.value_statement: Researchers often struggle to efficiently search NCBI GEO across multiple fields like organism, author, and dataset type. This skill handles query construction against the NCBI E-utilities API and returns structured summaries of matching datasets and profiles.
actual_capabilities: Search NCBI GEO DataSets (gds) and GEO Profiles (geoprofiles) databases
Filter results by organism, author, dataset type, publication date, and other GEO fields
Retrieve structured summaries including accession numbers, titles, taxa, and entry types
Construct valid NCBI E-utilities queries with proper field syntax and AND operators
Support user-provided email and API key for compliant NCBI API access with higher rate limits
Limit result counts to a user-specified maximum
limitations: Requires internet access to reach NCBI E-utilities API; no offline search capability
Does not download full GEO data files or supplementary materials, only search summaries
Limited to the search fields and aliases documented in references/query_fields.json
NCBI rate limits apply; API key recommended for high-volume searches

use_cases.title: Find datasets for a disease biomarker review
use_cases.description: A bioinformatician searches for gene expression datasets related to a specific disease (e.g., 'breast cancer') filtered by organism and dataset type to identify relevant public datasets for a meta-analysis.
use_cases.target_user: Bioinformatician or computational biologist conducting a systematic review of public gene expression data

use_cases.title: Retrieve datasets from a specific research group
use_cases.description: A researcher searches for all GEO datasets published by a known author in their field, filtered by organism and date range, to track publications and find related data for replication studies.
use_cases.target_user: Academic researcher tracking datasets published by specific labs or authors

use_cases.title: Search for datasets by a specific GEO accession
use_cases.description: A data curator looks up a known GEO accession number (e.g., GSE12345) to retrieve its official summary, taxonomy, and entry type for cataloging or cross-referencing in a database.
use_cases.target_user: Data curator or database administrator managing genomic data references

prompt_templates.title: Simple keyword search for GEO datasets
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the NCBI GEO database for datasets related to [your_topic]. Use the geo-search-api skill with the query '[your_topic]' and set --limit 10.
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner user who wants a quick search for datasets on a topic using default settings.

prompt_templates.title: Filtered search by organism and dataset type
prompt_templates.prompt: Search NCBI GEO for [your_topic] datasets filtered by organism '[organism_name]' and dataset type '[dataset_type]'. Use the geo-search-api skill with --filters '{"Organism": "[organism_name]", "DataSet Type": "[dataset_type]"}' and set --limit 10.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user who needs to narrow results by specific biological filters, such as finding human RNA-seq data.

prompt_templates.title: Author-specific search with API key for higher rate limits
prompt_templates.prompt: Search NCBI GEO for datasets by author '[author_name]' on the topic '[topic]'. Use the geo-search-api skill with query '[topic]' and --filters '{"Author": "[author_name]"}'. Include your --email and --api_key for compliant API access with higher rate limits.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher who wants to find all datasets published by a specific author and has an NCBI API key for better throughput.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive multi-field search with structured output
prompt_templates.prompt: Search NCBI GEO profiles for '[topic]' in organism '[organism_name]' by author '[author_name]' published after '[date]'. Use the geo-search-api skill with --db geoprofiles and --filters '{"Organism": "[organism_name]", "Author": "[author_name]", "Publication Date": "[date]"}'. Provide your --email for NCBI compliance. Parse the returned summaries and create a markdown table with accession, title, taxon, and entry type columns.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user who needs a structured cross-filtered search of GEO Profiles with formatted output for reporting.

output_examples.input: Search GEO datasets for 'diabetes' in Homo sapiens filtered by expression profiling by array, limit 5.
output_examples.output: Found 3 datasets matching your search. Top result: GSE12345 - 'Expression analysis of pancreatic islets in type 2 diabetes' (Homo sapiens, expression profiling by array, 2024). Includes accession number, title, taxon, entry type, and publication date for each result.

output_examples.input: Search GEO profiles for 'BRCA1' in Homo sapiens by author 'Smith J', limit 10.
output_examples.output: Retrieved 8 profile results for BRCA1 expression in Homo sapiens from author Smith J. Results include individual profile summaries with gene-level expression data points, detected taxon, and source publication identifiers.

output_examples.input: Search GEO datasets for 'GSE12345' to retrieve the official record summary.
output_examples.output: Single dataset record retrieved: Accession GSE12345 - 'Transcriptomic profiling of lung cancer samples' (Homo sapiens, expression profiling by high throughput sequencing, 2024). Summary includes experimental design overview, sample count, platform technology, and associated publications.

best_practices: Always provide your email address with --email when querying NCBI E-utilities to comply with NCBI usage policies and enable higher request rate limits.
Use specific search terms and filters rather than broad queries to reduce result volume and improve relevance. Combine organism, dataset type, and author filters for targeted searches.
Set --limit to a reasonable number (10-20) for interactive searches. For bulk retrieval, consider running multiple focused queries rather than one very large query.
anti_patterns: Do not skip providing an email address when running queries. NCBI E-utilities may block requests that lack contact information or violate rate limits.
Do not fabricate GEO results or dataset summaries. The skill retrieves real metadata from NCBI. If the API returns an error or empty results, report this accurately rather than inventing data.
Do not use complex or unvalidated filter values without checking references/query_fields.json for supported field names and aliases. Invalid field names will produce empty or incorrect results.

faq.question: What databases can this skill search?
faq.answer: This skill searches the NCBI GEO database through two sub-databases: 'gds' (Gene Expression Omnibus DataSets) and 'geoprofiles' (individual gene expression profiles).

faq.question: Do I need an NCBI API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, an API key is optional. NCBI E-utilities work without one, but with lower rate limits (about 3 requests per second without a key versus 10 with a key). You must provide an email address for NCBI compliance.

faq.question: What search fields are available for filtering?
faq.answer: Supported fields include Organism, Author, Title, Description, GEO Accession, MeSH Terms, Publication Date, Dataset Type, Entry Type, and more. See references/query_fields.json for the full list with field aliases.

faq.question: Can this skill download full GEO data files?
faq.answer: No, this skill only retrieves search summaries and metadata from NCBI. It does not download supplementary files, raw data, or full dataset archives.

faq.question: What output format does the skill return?
faq.answer: The skill returns structured JSON output with result count and an array of dataset or profile summaries. Each summary includes accession number, title, description, taxon, entry type, and publication date.

faq.question: What happens if my search returns no results?
faq.answer: The skill returns a result with count 0 and an empty results array. Check that your search terms are correct and that your filter field names match the supported values in references/query_fields.json.
